affinity.description:
Affinity for pod assignment
For more information: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity
affinity.title: affinity

pipelines.description:
Legacy global pipelines — MUST stay empty: pipelines are per-tenant now (see
`tenants.platform.pipelines` below and the tenant ConfigMaps), and a non-empty
list fails application startup by design.
pipelines.title: pipelines

secretRef.description:
Resolver for `${secretRef:<key>}` sink-credential indirection; "k8s-secret" reads the
tenant's own Secret `auditflow-tenant-<tenantId>-creds` (requires rbac.create), "env" reads
`AUDITFLOW_TENANT_<TENANTID>_<KEY>` environment variables
secretRef.title: secretRef

local.description: Local URL for the sink service
local.title: local

service.description: Service name and namespace for the sink
service.title: service

data.description: Redis connection params (backing store for the idempotency/dedup service; auditflow.idempotency.store=redis)

host.description: Redis host; defaults to the shared tools Redis. Override for your own Redis in non-local installs.
host.title: host

data.title: data

host.description: RabbitMQ host; resolves to the bundled subchart service when rabbitmq.enabled, else set your broker host
host.title: host

tenants.description:
Tenant model (silo isolation) — the chart pins the Kubernetes-native modes; the
application's built-in defaults are the cluster-free ones (local-dir / in-memory / env).
Keys below are exact Spring property paths (

max-in-flight-per-tenant.description: Max concurrently-processed events per tenant on the consumer side (fairness layer 2)
max-in-flight-per-tenant.title: max-in-flight-per-tenant

backend.description:
Per-tenant ingest rate-limit backend; "redis" is correct with >1 replica (the
application default "in-memory" is single-replica only)
backend.title: backend

mode.description:
Tenant config source; "gitops-configmap" watches ConfigMaps labelled `auditflow.io/tenant`
in the release namespace (requires rbac.create), "local-dir" polls a mounted directory
mode.title: mode

tenants.title: tenants

local.description: Local URL for the transformer service
local.title: local

service.description: Service name and namespace for the transformer
service.title: service

autoscaling.description: This section is for setting up autoscaling more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/
autoscaling.title: autoscaling

chart-libs.description: Values passed to the chart-libs library dependency (present so the generated schema accepts the key Helm injects for the dependency)
chart-libs.title: chart-libs

env.description: Environment variables to add to the container
env.title: env

gateway.description: Gateway routes published by this module (rendered by chart-libs.gateway-routes)

authPolicy.description:
Auth-policy publication. NOTE: the traefik-authproxy no longer
live-discovers per-module policies via /.well-known/auth-policy — it loads a
generated routes manifest (charts/api-gateway/routes/) and asks the
central Cerbos PDP for every decision. This block is retained for backward
compatibility and is a no-op for edge enforcement.

basePath.description: External base path prepended to the module's OpenAPI paths; defaults to <prefix>/api/v1
basePath.title: basePath

enabled.description: Publish this module's auth policy to the gateway ACS
enabled.title: enabled

authPolicy.title: authPolicy

enabled.description: Publish this module's routes on the Traefik gateway
enabled.title: enabled

parentRefs.description: Gateway API parent Gateway(s) this module's HTTPRoute attaches to
parentRefs.title: parentRefs

sharedMiddlewares.description: Names of the shared middlewares provided by the api-gateway chart (fullnameOverride: gateway-common)
sharedMiddlewares.title: sharedMiddlewares

gateway.title: gateway

global.description: Global values shared across Labs64.IO charts and Bitnami subcharts

allowInsecureImages.description: Required by Bitnami subcharts when images are pulled from bitnamilegacy (image substitution guard)
allowInsecureImages.title: allowInsecureImages

global.title: global

timeout.description: Max time Spring Boot waits for in-flight work before forced shutdown
timeout.title: timeout

image.description: This sets the container image more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/

pullPolicy.description: This sets the pull policy for images.
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

tag.description: Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
tag.title: tag

image.title: image

imagePullSecrets.description: This is for the secrets for pulling an image from a private repository more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

preStopDrainSeconds.description: preStop sleep (seconds) so Traefik/kube-proxy deregister the pod before shutdown; 0 disables
preStopDrainSeconds.title: preStopDrainSeconds

livenessProbe.description: This is to setup the liveness probes more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/
livenessProbe.title: livenessProbe

nameOverride.description: This is to override the chart name.
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

networkPolicy.description: NetworkPolicy: allow ingress from Traefik and same-namespace pods only (rendered by chart-libs.networkpolicy)

toolsEgress.description:
Tools-namespace destinations this service needs (name + port pairs); rendered as
scoped egress rules — NOT a blanket allow to the whole tools namespace — to preserve
database-per-service isolation.
toolsEgress.title: toolsEgress

networkPolicy.title: networkPolicy

nodeSelector.description:
Node labels for pod assignment
For more information: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/
nodeSelector.title: nodeSelector

observability.description:
Observability is an infrastructure concern: the same images run with or without it.
When enabled, the backend's bundled OTel Java Agent is activated via JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS and
the Python sidecars start under opentelemetry-instrument (triggered by the OTLP endpoint env).
Java metrics stay on Micrometer via /actuator/prometheus (prometheus.io/* pod annotations
are added automatically when enabled).

enabled.description: Enable runtime instrumentation (traces + logs OTLP, Prometheus-annotation metrics scrape)
enabled.title: enabled

otlpEndpoint.description: OTLP endpoint of the OpenTelemetry Collector
otlpEndpoint.title: otlpEndpoint

observability.title: observability

podAnnotations.description:
This is for setting Kubernetes Annotations to a Pod.
For more information checkout: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
podAnnotations.title: podAnnotations

podLabels.description:
This is for setting Kubernetes Labels to a Pod.
For more information checkout: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
podLabels.title: podLabels

rbac.description:
RBAC configuration (create RBAC resources: Role, RoleBinding)
Least-privilege, namespace-scoped: the tenant-config informer (tenants.source.mode=
gitops-configmap) needs ConfigMap get/list/watch; the secretRef resolver
(secretRef.resolver=k8s-secret) needs Secret get; kubernetes service discovery
(transformer/sink) needs Service get.
rbac.title: rbac

readinessProbe.description: This is to setup the readiness probes more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/
readinessProbe.title: readinessProbe

replicaCount.description: This will set the replicaset count more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/replicaset/
replicaCount.title: replicaCount

resources.description:
Resource limits and requests for the container
For production, it's recommended to set both requests and limits
resources.title: resources

secrets.description:
Secret data to be used as environment variables (delivered via envFrom).
External installs supply broker credentials here, e.g.
  SPRING_RABBITMQ_USERNAME / SPRING_RABBITMQ_PASSWORD.
When rabbitmq.enabled=true the chart adds these keys automatically from rabbitmq.auth.
Keys you set here take precedence over the bundled-dep keys. On helm upgrade the Secret is deleted and recreated (hook-managed).
Note: the Secret is hook-managed (pre-install) and survives helm uninstall.
secrets.title: secrets

service.description: This is for setting up a service more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/

port.description: This sets the ports more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#field-spec-ports
port.title: port

type.description: This sets the service type more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types
type.title: type

service.title: service

serviceAccount.description: This section builds out the service account more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/service-accounts/

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account
annotations.title: annotations

automount.description: Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
automount.title: automount

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created
create.title: create

name.description:
The name of the service account to use.
If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
name.title: name

serviceAccount.title: serviceAccount

resources.description: Resource requests/limits for the sink sidecar (lightweight Python/FastAPI service).
resources.title: resources

service.description: This is for setting up a service more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/

port.description: This sets the ports more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#field-spec-ports
port.title: port

service.title: service

slo.description: SLO recording rules and dashboards (rendered by chart-libs.slo.*)
slo.title: slo

startupProbe.description:
Startup probe (rendered by chart-libs.startupProbe): guards slow cold starts
(Spring Boot + OTel Java Agent) so the liveness probe never kills a still-booting
pod. Max boot budget = failureThreshold * periodSeconds.
startupProbe.title: startupProbe

tenants.description:
Tenant provisioning shipped by the chart (rendered as `auditflow.io/tenant`-labelled
ConfigMaps consumed live by the gitops-configmap source — see templates/tenant-configmaps.yaml).

additional.description:
Additional tenants to provision with the release: a list of full tenant documents
({tenantId, enabled, quota, pipelines}); each renders as its own labelled ConfigMap.
Tenants can also be onboarded at any time by applying such a ConfigMap out-of-band (GitOps).
additional.title: additional

platform.description:
The reserved `_platform` pseudo-tenant for tenantless/platform events. Shipped by
default so platform events flow out of the box; K8s object name/label use the sanitized
`platform`, the body keeps the canonical `_platform`.

burst.description: Per-tenant ingest rate limit: burst capacity
burst.title: burst

pipelines.description: The platform tenant's pipelines (same shape as the former global auditflow.pipelines)
pipelines.title: pipelines

rateLimitPerSec.description: Per-tenant ingest rate limit (token bucket): sustained events/second
rateLimitPerSec.title: rateLimitPerSec

platform.title: platform

tenants.title: tenants

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description:
Graceful shutdown: drain in-flight requests / message handlers on rolling
updates and scale-in. Keep terminationGracePeriodSeconds > preStop + gracefulShutdown.timeout.
terminationGracePeriodSeconds.title: terminationGracePeriodSeconds

tolerations.description:
Tolerations for pod assignment
For more information: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/
tolerations.title: tolerations

resources.description: Resource requests/limits for the transformer sidecar (lightweight Python/FastAPI service).
resources.title: resources

service.description: This is for setting up a service more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/

port.description: This sets the ports more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#field-spec-ports
port.title: port

service.title: service

volumeMounts.description: Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts.title: volumeMounts

volumes.description: Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes.title: volumes
